claims.claim: El Al economy carry-on is 1x8kg (56x45x25 cm) plus a personal item; Economy Classic/Flex include 1x23kg checked; since May 2025 Lite fares to Europe/UAE must gate-check the carry-on (free), leaving only a 38x30x18 cm personal item in the cabin; Business carry-on is 16kg.
claims.raw_snippet: Economy carry-on: 8 kg, 56x45x25 cm, plus a personal item. Economy Lite to Europe and the UAE: carry-on must be checked at the gate; only a personal item up to 38x30x18 cm is allowed in the cabin.

claims.claim: El Al Matmid has five tiers; since April 2025 status is earned via a revenue-linked Diamonds currency and re-qualified every 12 months, and the soft-landing grace tier was eliminated.
claims.raw_snippet: Matmid frequent flyer club tiers; status earned through the Diamonds revenue-based currency and re-qualified annually.

claims.claim: Israir charges for everything except a personal item: carry-on 10kg $30 advance / $40 airport; 1st checked bag 23kg $65 advance (from 1 May 2026) / $100 airport; 2nd bag $80; bags 3-5 $120 each; overweight (24-32kg) +$20 advance / +$70 airport.
claims.raw_snippet: Israir baggage: carry-on $30 in advance, $40 at the airport; first checked bag $65 per direction in advance (effective 1 May 2026), $100 at the airport; additional bags $80 and $120.

claims.claim: Arkia international baggage: carry-on 7kg free; trolley 8kg $20 advance / $25 airport; checked bag 20kg $45 advance / $90 airport; excess $10 per kg.
claims.raw_snippet: Arkia checked baggage 20 kg costs $45 in advance and $90 at the airport; trolley bag $20; excess baggage $10 per kg.

claims.claim: Wizz Air resumed TLV operations on 28 May 2026, reconnecting Tel Aviv with hubs including London, Budapest, Rome, Bucharest, Larnaca, Milan, and Athens; base fares include only a 40x30x20 cm personal item.
claims.raw_snippet: Wizz Air resumed flights to Tel Aviv on 28 May 2026; base fare includes one small personal item, cabin and checked bags are paid add-ons.

claims.claim: ETIAS travel authorization is becoming mandatory for visa-exempt nationals (including Israelis) before entering the Schengen area, and EES replaces passport stamping with biometric border registration.
claims.raw_snippet: ETIAS is a travel authorisation for visa-exempt non-EU nationals to travel to Schengen countries; it is linked to the traveller's passport and must be obtained before travel.

claims.claim: The Israel Airports Authority advises arriving 3 hours before international departures from Ben Gurion because of layered security screening.
claims.raw_snippet: Passengers are advised to arrive at Ben Gurion Airport 3 hours before an international flight.

claims.claim: Recent Expedia data put the cheapest day to fly mid-week (Tuesday, around 14% below the peak day domestically) with weekend departures at a premium; shifting dates by 1-2 days can save 30%+.
claims.raw_snippet: Expedia found the cheapest day to fly is Tuesday, with travelers saving about 14 percent on average versus the most expensive day; weekend departures cost more.

claims.claim: Google Flights (Israel), Skyscanner, KAYAK, Issta, and Lametayel are all operating flight-comparison platforms accessible to Israeli users in Hebrew.
claims.raw_snippet: Google Flights lets users search and compare flights with locale set to Israel (gl=IL, hl=he), including AI Flight Deals and fare-history tracking.

claims.claim: Israeli travelers track error fares and flash deals through secret-flights sites such as secretflights.co.il and through Telegram/Facebook deal channels, which rarely appear on the big aggregators.
claims.raw_snippet: secretflights.co.il (טיסות סודיות) surfaces mispriced and error fares from Israel that do not appear on standard comparison engines.
